title: South-facing ranger walk cycle
prompt: Use the approved identity master of a compact forest ranger with a moss-green hood, tan tunic, brown boots, a small round wooden buckler on the left arm, and a short iron sword in the right hand. Keep the ranger facing south and preserve face, body proportions, clothing, equipment hands, palette, outline, and foot root. Create a grounded walk cycle with ordered motion beats: left-foot contact, left support down, left passing, right-foot contact, right support down, right passing. The support foot alternates, each foot visibly contacts the same baseline, and body motion follows the gait; this is locomotion, not six unrelated poses. Crisp 32x32 pixel art per frame, transparent background, no text, no frame border.

beats.semantic: left foot lands on the baseline while the right leg trails
left leg accepts weight and the body reaches its lowest point
right foot passes the planted left leg as the body rises
right foot lands on the baseline while the left leg trails
right leg accepts weight and the body reaches its lowest point
left foot passes the planted right leg and closes toward the first contact
